turns.prompt: We're kicking off the 'orderkit' Python project (pure stdlib, code under src/). Let me establish the project conventions and decisions up front — please internalize these, we'll rely on them throughout: (1) every new Python file must begin with the exact header line `# WH-CONVENTION v3`; (2) money is ALWAYS integer cents — we hit nasty float rounding bugs in prod, so never use float for money anywhere; (3) the maximum line items per order is 100 — note we deliberately raised this from an earlier 50; use a constant MAX_LINE_ITEMS = 100; (4) order status strings are UPPERCASE like 'SUBMITTED' — this supersedes an older lowercase-status convention we used to have. To get us started, create src/inventory.py with a pure function stock_level(records, sku) that sums quantities for a SKU across a list of record dicts. Keep it simple.

Create src/orders_v2.py with a PURE function create_order(customer, items), where items is a list of (product_id, price_cents, qty) tuples. It must: enforce the maximum line-items cap; compute the order total in integer cents; and return a dict {"customer": customer, "status": <submitted-state>, "total_cents": <int>, "line_count": <int>}. No database — just return the dict. Follow every project convention and decision we established for orderkit.
